title: Aftermath - Let's Play - Part Four
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TMvgKj-2Z9k

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns from Thanksgiving and admits two things have eaten his evenings: *King's Dilemma* (he is captivated) and prep for an upcoming Myth video series. He's back at Aftermath, deep inside Enjo's mechanical guts.

This chapter introduces three new **environment** rules at once: **heavy objects** (the Snoodles count, requiring a Group Task to drag across any white-dotted line); **low visibility** (every ranged attack becomes single-space, neutralising Mesaiya's toothpick crossbow); and **forced movement** (arrow spaces shove you, the Snoodles, and the clue tokens *one square in the marked direction* every time a threat card is placed). Daniel makes the deliberate decision to push the Snoodles *off the cliff* — losing his clean-mission win, but progressing to the boss-fight chamber underneath, which he genuinely wants to experience.

The Hunt then fires: the calamity roll spawns a six-strong cockroach swarm at the cliff edge that immediately picks up the Snoodles' chase. The roaches will follow the heroes into the next map, hostile-situation flagged. Daniel calls out Plaid Hat's roach minis as *colour-printed and labelled* — a small but real improvement over the unlabelled tokens in Mice and Mystics.

----

Daniel chooses to *fail forward* on purpose — pushing the Snoodles off the cliff so he can experience the boss-fight chapter. Do you make decisions like that on a first-play, or do you only sacrifice optimal outcomes for content on a second?
